market_regime: Risk-on tech-led rotation: QQQ +3.4% 20d, VIX -9.8%, XLF +4%, GLD -9.5% — lean AI infrastructure, defense/drone reshoring, and oversold quality compounders; FOMC June 18 is the nearest gatekeeper

scanner_blind_spots.reason: Scanner missed the best AI power infrastructure play — GEV sells gas turbines and grid equipment that power AI data centers with a $42B backlog and 3-5 year lead times. Morgan Stanley target $1,350. Scanner screens for traditional fundamentals and cannot detect the AI power constraint mosaic.

Scanner missed NVIDIA's preferred liquid cooling partner for Blackwell GPU deployments. Every hyperscaler AI data center upgrade mandates Vertiv thermal systems. $7.3B backlog +30% YoY. The NVIDIA partnership creates embedded design wins not visible in fundamental screens.

Scanner missed the institutional triple convergence: Klarman (Baupost top-5), Elliott (5.6% activist), and CEO Sommer ($27.5M personal buy) all entered simultaneously in Q1 2026. No fundamental screen catches three of the most credible investors in the world converging on one name.

Scanner missed RSI 25.85 extreme technical dislocation against EPS +48% YoY fundamental strength. Most oversold S&P 500 member while growing earnings nearly 50% is a textbook contrarian signal requiring cross-referencing RSI data against actual earnings results.

Co-Chairman Hirsch purchased $3M personally on June 11 (5 days before this assessment) — a very fresh Tier 1 insider signal the scanner's weekly fundamental data cannot capture. Private AUM +22% with retail democratization via Everest platform represents significant TAM expansion.

Scanner appeared to underweight Cognizant's extreme value (12.7x Fwd P/E vs IT median 20x) combined with CFO insider buy and AI engineering services growing 40% YoY. The combination of deep value + insider conviction + AI growth is a discovery-layer insight.

Discovery: Down 50%+ YTD on guidance cuts driven by WATCHMAN franchise share loss and EP competition — but 27 of 31 analysts still rate it Buy with a consensus target of ~$78 (65% upside from ~$47); the selloff may be overshooting if competitive headwinds stabilize in H2.
value_picks.analyst_take: Fastest organic revenue growth in large-cap med device at 14% YoY; EP/Farapulse PFA launch strong; Watchman FLX gaining share

catalysts_general: Athabasca Basin lowest-cost globally; 40% contracted book restoring 2026-2028; AI data center operators contracting nuclear directly. Source: Tier 4 (Reuters nuclear renaissance)

Discovery: Kazakhstan (39% of world uranium supply) signaled 2026 production reduction and is building a strategic reserve, while US/EU utilities are urgently contracting Western supply ahead of the planned 2028 Russian uranium import ban — Cameco as the largest Western producer is the primary re-contracting beneficiary with spot near $86/lb.
value_picks.analyst_take: Largest public uranium miner; AI nuclear power renaissance (Amazon/Microsoft/Google PPAs); 40% contract book re-pricing at higher rates

catalysts_general: Consumer goods destocking ended; gross margin expanding from 32% to 36-38% target; nutrition asset divestiture funding debt paydown. Source: Tier 3 (HSBC research)

Discovery: Board member Paul Fribourg bought 273,500 shares for ~$20.3M in the days immediately after IFF announced the $4.3B sale of its Food Ingredients business to CVC Capital Partners (May 29, 2026) — insider putting $20M behind a deleveraging/focus story where ~$3.8B net cash proceeds will reduce debt and fund buybacks.
value_picks.analyst_take: Destocking headwind fully behind; gross margin recovery to 36%+ target; divestiture simplifies; HSBC Buy $95 target

catalysts_general: Worldpay divestiture complete 2023; pure-play banking infrastructure; $3.5B buyback authorized; core banking modernization structural demand. Source: Tier 4 (Reuters FIS)
value_picks.analyst_take: 9.2x Fwd P/E for mission-critical banking software; Worldpay overhang removed; $3.5B buyback at trough valuation

catalysts_general: SEPHIENCE (sepiapterin) PKU launched Q1 2026. Citi Buy upgrade with $108 target. Gene therapy DMD Phase 3. WARNING: June 20 cytisinicline PDUFA is ACHV not PTCT. Source: Tier 3 (Citi research)

Discovery: Citigroup upgraded from Neutral to Buy on June 9, 2026 and raised target to $108 (from $75, a 44% increase); TD Cowen and Morgan Stanley also bullish; company reported record Q1 product revenue and raised full-year 2026 guidance on strong early commercial demand for Sephience (phenylketonuria treatment).
value_picks.analyst_take: SEPHIENCE commercial ramp in PKU; Citi upgraded to Buy/$108 (41% upside). NOTE: June 20 PDUFA in context data belongs to ACHV (Achieve Life Sciences), NOT PTCT — scanner data error

catalysts_general: $874M US Army contract; Marine Corps Valkyrie selection; $63B FY2026 drone budget. Earnings June 29 — wait for post-earnings reaction. Source: Tier 2 (AeroVironment press releases)

Discovery: The FY2026 defense authorization bill earmarks an estimated $63B for drone/unmanned systems — more than 6x prior enacted levels — and AeroVironment's Switchblade loitering munitions and Raven/Puma ISR drones are combat-validated in active conflict zones, positioning it as a direct contract beneficiary of the Pentagon's Replicator autonomous systems initiative.
value_picks.analyst_take: Record $3.5B backlog; 93% revenue backed by firm orders; $63B drone budget; WAIT for June 29 earnings before entry

catalysts_general: Amazon historic nuclear colocation PPA at Cumulus Data (Susquehanna plant) — first direct AI nuclear PPA. Emerged from Chapter 11 2024. Post-bankruptcy single plant risk. Source: Tier 2 (Amazon/Talen press release)
value_picks.analyst_take: Only public nuclear colocation data center power company; Amazon PPA at Susquehanna plant = proof of concept for AI nuclear co-location

catalysts_general: AWS $29.3B Q1 +17% YoY; advertising $13.9B +21%; $200B 2026 CapEx commitment compresses near-term FCF dramatically. Context FCF $9.8B appears stale pre-CapEx-acceleration. Source: Tier 4 (Reuters Q1 earnings)
value_picks.analyst_take: AWS world's largest cloud +17%; advertising +21%; $200B 2026 CapEx creating 5-7 year moat — NOTE: FCF compressed to ~$1.2B (context $9.8B appears stale)

avoid_picks.analyst_take: Goldman SELL rating; management Q3 2025 commentary described AI tax prep as cannibalization threat to TurboTax; 32x Fwd P/E for declining unit volume — scanner's strongest false positive
Analyst consensus target $101 is 20% BELOW current price $127.06 — professional analysts say overvalued; AI language learning (ChatGPT) is structurally superior for motivated adult learners
Debt/equity 5,293x + declining revenue YoY + 25.2% short interest — capital structure is disqualifying regardless of market research monopoly

Ryan Cohen eBay bid is theater — acquiring a structurally declining used-goods marketplace by issuing GME shares would be immediately shorted by every institutional investor; underlying business irrelevant
Class action lawsuit alleging misleading revenue statements + $300M dilutive stock offering + no path to profitability + voice AI is commoditized
GF Value $129.43 vs current price $388.92 = 3x intrinsic value; analyst consensus target AT current price (no upside); paying dot-com euphoria multiples for 2026 fundamentals
P/S >16x (dot-com peak for capital equipment); analyst consensus target $525 is BELOW current price $585 — two independent valuation methods both say overvalued
Correct drone defense thesis but wrong price: GF Value $27-55 vs $57 current; insiders SELLING $12-13M with zero buying; overlap signal found the right story at the wrong price
Down only 10.2% from 52W high; Fwd P/E still >35x; paying near-peak price for world's most loved chip company — wait for >25% correction from high; prefer AVGO at current prices
24% workforce RIF simultaneously with 8% FY revenue guide cut = structural contraction not cost optimization; AI disruption to freelance platform accelerating
